fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A bottom type is the universal subtype and an uninhabitated type that has no values and therefore no constructors. It is often denoted by the symbol \bot . If a function promises to return a bottom type, it will never terminate successfully (this includes errors and exceptions). As such, the bottom type can be used to represent the type of expressions that never return a value. Because \bot is a subtype of everything, you can safely use a bottom-type expression anywhere any other type is expected.

A top type is the universal supertype that encompasses all values as its inhabitants. It is often denoted by the symbol \top . A top type can be useful for representing the type of expressions that can have any value or where specific type information has been abstracted away.
